subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What does the acronym GSM stand for in the context of mobile communications?
explanation: According to the source topics under 'Acronyms & Terminologies', GSM is a fundamental cellular standard which stands for Global System for Mobile Communications.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Which of the following is the correct full meaning of the acronym SIM?
explanation: The source lists 'SIM' under Acronyms & Terminologies. SIM stands for Subscriber Identity Module, which holds subscriber network data.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is the full meaning of the acronym SMS?
explanation: Based on standard acronyms in the source text, SMS stands for Short Message Service, widely used for text messaging.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What does the acronym IMEI represent?
explanation: IMEI stands for International Mobile Equipment Identity, a critical acronym listed in the syllabus for identifying mobile devices.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What does PIN stand for in mobile phone security?
explanation: Listed under acronyms, PIN stands for Personal Identification Number, typically used as a primary security barrier for SIM cards.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Which category of mobile phones is typically defined by running advanced operating systems like Android or iOS?
explanation: The source identifies 'modern smartphones' explicitly in conjunction with Android/iOS operating systems under 'Types of Phones'.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Which universally recognized USSD code is used to view a mobile phone's IMEI number?
explanation: The source text explicitly highlights '*#06#' as the USSD code for checking the IMEI of a mobile device.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is the primary function of a power bank accessory?

options.b: To provide portable electrical charging for the device's battery
options.d: To act as an external speaker

explanation: Listed under 'Phone Accessories', a power bank is primarily a portable battery used for charging mobile devices on the go.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: According to mobile phone form factors, a flip phone is also commonly referred to as a(n):
explanation: The source lists 'flip/clamshell' together under 'Types of Phones', indicating these terms refer to the same hinged design.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is the primary purpose of a Bluetooth earpiece accessory?

options.a: To provide physical connection to a computer
options.b: To allow hands-free voice calls and wireless audio listening

explanation: Listed under 'Phone Accessories', earpieces and Bluetooth devices primarily facilitate hands-free audio and calls.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What does the acronym CDMA represent in cellular networks?
explanation: Listed under acronyms in the syllabus, CDMA stands for Code Division Multiple Access, an alternative cellular technology to GSM.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is the full meaning of the term PUK in GSM terminology?
explanation: PUK stands for Personal Unblocking Key, used to unlock a SIM card when the PIN has been incorrectly entered.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: How does MMS fundamentally differ from SMS?

options.a: MMS works over Bluetooth only, while SMS uses cellular networks
options.b: MMS is strictly for longer text documents, while SMS handles short texts
options.c: MMS supports multimedia content like pictures and audio, whereas SMS is restricted to text
options.d: MMS is an acronym for Micro Messaging Service

explanation: MMS stands for Multimedia Messaging Service, which supports media attachments (photos, videos, audio), unlike the text-only Short Message Service (SMS).

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Which mobile phone form factor features a mechanism where the screen pushes upward to reveal the physical keypad beneath?
explanation: As noted under 'Types of Phones', a slider phone has two main sections that slide past each other on rails.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Which type of phone takes the physical shape of a single block with no moving mechanisms or hinges?
explanation: A 'bar' phone (or candybar) consists of a single unarticulated block. It is explicitly distinguished from slider and flip types in the syllabus.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is the primary function of an OTG (On-The-Go) cable for a smartphone?

options.a: To connect the phone to an external high-definition monitor via HDMI
options.b: To enable the phone to host standard USB peripherals like flash drives and mice
options.c: To convert AC mains power to lower DC voltage
options.d: To permanently boost the phone's internal antenna gain

explanation: Under 'Phone Accessories', OTG (USB On-The-Go) cables are used to switch a smartphone into host mode, allowing standard USB devices to be connected.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: According to the source, which of the following lists the major telecom service providers operating in Nigeria?
explanation: The source explicitly names 'MTN, Airtel, Glo, 9Mobile in Nigeria' as the major service providers.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is a practical advantage of the 900MHz frequency band over the 1800MHz frequency band in mobile communications?

options.a: It supports much higher data transfer speeds
options.b: It offers better penetration through buildings and travels longer distances
options.c: It accommodates a vastly larger number of concurrent users
options.d: It requires significantly more cellular towers

explanation: Lower frequencies, like 900MHz, provide a larger coverage area and superior penetration through obstacles like building walls compared to 1800MHz.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What are USSD codes typically utilized for by a mobile phone subscriber?

options.a: Sending encrypted multimedia files over Wi-Fi
options.b: Formatting the internal storage of the device
options.c: Directly checking balances, contacting customer care, and accessing network menus
options.d: Automatically updating the Android or iOS software

explanation: The syllabus mentions USSD codes specifically for 'checking balances, customer care, and IMEI (*#06#)'.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: When a subscriber is prompted by their device to input a PUK code, what security event has typically just occurred?

options.a: The user entered an incorrect USSD command
options.b: The phone's main processor experienced an overheat warning
options.c: The SIM PIN was entered incorrectly three times, locking the SIM
options.d: The user attempted to download a restricted application

explanation: The Personal Unblocking Key (PUK) is requested by the SIM card system strictly after the standard PIN has been failed (usually 3 times).

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: How many consecutive incorrect attempts at a PIN usually trigger the mobile device to block the SIM and ask for a PUK?
explanation: Under standard GSM security architectures (covering PIN and PUK concepts in the syllabus), a PIN gets blocked after exactly 3 unsuccessful attempts.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: In which primary geographic region was the 1900MHz mobile frequency band historically deployed instead of 900/1800MHz?
explanation: The 1900MHz band (also known as PCS1900) was primarily deployed in North America, while Europe, Africa, and Asia generally adopted 900MHz and 1800MHz.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What electrical task does a standard mobile phone charger perform when plugged into a wall outlet?

options.a: It converts high-voltage AC from the mains into low-voltage DC for the battery
options.b: It steps up battery DC to high AC voltage for transmission
options.c: It strictly acts as an antenna booster for the charging cable
options.d: It directly transmits AC power into the lithium-ion battery

explanation: Under 'Phone Accessories', chargers must contain a transformer/rectifier circuit to convert high alternating current (AC) to direct current (DC) safely for phone electronics.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Android and iOS are prominently classified as what type of software on modern mobile devices?
explanation: The syllabus points to '(Android/iOS)' to describe modern smartphones, which are the sophisticated operating systems that manage the hardware and software.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Does an IMEI primarily identify the telecommunication subscriber (the user) or the actual physical mobile equipment?

options.a: It exclusively identifies the physical hardware equipment
options.b: It exclusively identifies the subscriber billing account
options.c: It identifies the network's local cell tower
options.d: It identifies the specific phone number attached to the SIM

explanation: IMEI (International Mobile Equipment Identity) uniquely identifies the handset/hardware. The SIM and IMSI identify the subscriber.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: At a core technological level, what defines the difference between GSM and CDMA networking?

options.a: GSM uses wired connections, whereas CDMA is entirely wireless
options.b: GSM separates channels strictly by unique encryption codes, while CDMA uses standard SIM cards for timing
options.c: GSM primarily divides transmission by distinct time slots and frequencies (TDMA/FDMA), while CDMA uses mathematical spread-spectrum codes across the same frequency
options.d: GSM operates only on 1900MHz, while CDMA operates only on 900MHz

explanation: GSM and CDMA are competing original network standards. GSM uses Time Division Multiple Access (TDMA), isolating users by time and frequency. CDMA isolates users via code division.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Why do telecom engineers implement the 1800MHz GSM band in densely populated city areas rather than relying exclusively on the 900MHz band?

options.a: Because 1800MHz penetrates thick concrete walls more effectively than 900MHz
options.b: Because the 1800MHz band naturally prevents unauthorized eavesdropping
options.c: Because higher frequencies like 1800MHz provide more available bandwidth to handle a higher capacity of simultaneous calls
options.d: Because older feature phones are not technically compatible with 900MHz

explanation: Higher frequency bands (1800/1900MHz) cover shorter distances but offer a larger spectrum slice, thereby granting vastly superior user capacity in dense urban centers.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: If a user forgets their PIN, inputs it wrong 3 times, and then subsequently inputs the PUK code incorrectly 10 consecutive times, what is the exact hardware consequence?

options.a: The mobile phone processor burns out and must be replaced
options.b: The SIM card is permanently disabled/ruined and must be physically replaced by the provider
options.c: The network temporarily suspends the account for 24 hours
options.d: The phone prompts for an administrator recovery password to unlock the SIM

explanation: A strict security feature of the GSM standard is that 10 wrong PUK attempts permanently fry or 'brick' the SIM chip to prevent unauthorized cloning or access.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What is the fixed, standard digit length of a Personal Unblocking Key (PUK) code?
explanation: Standard PIN codes are generally 4 digits, but the Personal Unblocking Key (PUK) is mandated by GSM specifications to be an 8-digit numerical code.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: How many digits typically comprise a standard IMEI number assigned to a mobile phone?
explanation: The International Mobile Equipment Identity (IMEI) number is uniformly configured as a 15-digit number globally (though a 16th software-version digit sometimes exists, standard is 15).

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: At a hardware level, how does an OTG cable physically signal a mobile device's charging port to switch from acting as a 'client' to a 'USB Host'?

options.a: It applies 12 volts to the power pin
options.b: It grounds the micro-USB/USB-C ID pin, notifying the controller chip to supply power outwards
options.c: It sends a USSD code through the data line
options.d: It reverses the polarity of the data pins (D+ and D-)

explanation: An OTG (On-The-Go) cable features a wired short between the ID (Sense) pin and the Ground pin. The smartphone detects this short and transitions to acting as the USB Host.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: A modern mobile phone designed for truly global international roaming is often designated as a 'quad-band' device. Which four frequencies does it support?
explanation: To seamlessly roam between European/African grids (900/1800MHz) and American grids (850/1900MHz), quad-band devices support all four major GSM bands: 850, 900, 1800, and 1900 MHz.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Historically, what primary hardware trait distinguished original CDMA network devices from standard GSM devices regarding user subscriber identity?

options.a: Original CDMA phones natively lacked removable SIM cards, with subscriber identity flashed directly into the phone's memory
options.b: Original CDMA devices utilized dual SIM card slots exclusively
options.c: Original CDMA devices used paper voucher cards for identity instead of microchips
options.d: Original CDMA devices required physically plugging the phone into a wall to swap users

explanation: While GSM mandated the Subscriber Identity Module (SIM) for portability, original CDMA networks (like Verizon/Sprint initially) programmed the Electronic Serial Number (ESN) directly, requiring carrier intervention to switch handsets.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: The acronym USSD, famously used for strings like *#06#, stands for which of the following terms?
explanation: USSD stands for Unstructured Supplementary Service Data, a protocol used by GSM phones to communicate with service providers.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: What fundamental networking mechanism defines the technological difference between USSD operations and SMS messaging?

options.a: USSD is restricted to internet connectivity, while SMS works over pure cellular waves
options.b: USSD establishes a responsive real-time open session between phone and server, while SMS employs a 'store-and-forward' methodology
options.c: SMS sends commands directly to cell towers, while USSD only interfaces directly with SIM files
options.d: SMS requires the input of the '#' symbol to function, unlike USSD

explanation: A USSD query opens an immediate two-way live session with the provider's server (crucial for menus/balances), whereas SMS sends messages that wait at a server center until the recipient device is available (store-and-forward).

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: The capacity metric for phone battery accessories, explicitly power banks, is predominantly measured using which unit?
explanation: Power banks and mobile batteries describe their total electrical charge capacity in $mAh$ (milliampere-hour), for instance, a 10000 mAh power bank.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Aside from aesthetic compactness, what primary mechanical and functional advantage did early clamshell phone designs provide over standard bar phones?

options.a: The clamshell form physically shielded the interior screen and keys when closed, negating the need for a software key-lock to prevent 'pocket-dialing'
options.b: The folding hinge inherently contained dual GSM antennas for double network reception
options.c: The mechanical folding motion acted to manually charge the battery slightly each time
options.d: They inherently operated purely on 1900MHz frequency bandwidth

explanation: A prominent practical engineering feature of the flip/clamshell design was its natural screen/key protection and elimination of accidental pocket dials simply by folding shut.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: Standard Bluetooth mobile earpieces and connectivity modules generally use 'Class 2' Bluetooth specifications. What is the approximate functional maximum range for this class?
explanation: Under 'Bluetooth devices', the most standard short-range classification utilized for standard mobile phone pairing (Class 2 Bluetooth) has an effective operating radius of 10 meters.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: The 1800MHz frequency band, commonly implemented alongside 900MHz for expanded capacity in GSM networks, is historically referred to by which specific technical acronym?
explanation: While the 1900MHz band is termed PCS, the 1800MHz band deployed mostly in Europe, Africa, and Asia to augment GSM-900 is technically designated as DCS (Digital Cellular System) 1800.

subject: Computer Hardware and GSM Repairs
topic: Introduction to Mobile Phones and Service Providers
question_text: If a subscriber imports a simple dual-band 'feature phone' manufactured exclusively to support 900MHz and 1800MHz from Nigeria to the United States, what failure will almost certainly occur?

options.a: The USSD interface will revert exclusively to the French language
options.b: The device's power charger will blow a fuse instantly upon entry
options.c: The device will fail to locate cellular signal because the USA primarily employs 850MHz and 1900MHz bands
options.d: The phone will force the CDMA protocol to override GSM automatically

explanation: Because North America traditionally uses the 850MHz and 1900MHz bands, older hardware only equipped with European/African dual-band modems (900/1800MHz) lack the physical antennas/chips to communicate with US cell towers.
